title: callista rotundata
intro: Callista rotundata, commonly known as the Himalayan vine, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-diabetic properties. The therapeutic actions of Callista rotundata include its ability to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Callista rotundata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Callista rotundata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, gastrointestinal issues, and skin irritation. In some cases, consuming the plant's leaves or roots can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin lesions, blisters, and even permanent scarring. Improper use of Callista rotundata can also trigger cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat and blood pressure fluctuations.
